seoTitle: Take and Share a Screenshot Inside Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Capture a clean screenshot of a Teams chat or presentation using Windows or Mac shortcuts, then paste it right back into the conversation.
intro: Capturing a slide, a piece of chat text, or a meeting moment inside Teams doesn't require a separate screenshot app — your operating system's built-in shortcuts handle it cleanly. This guide covers capturing, marking up, and pasting a screenshot back into a Teams conversation.

steps.heading: Set up your target view
steps.body: Arrange the chat thread, video gallery, or presentation content on screen before you capture.

steps.heading: Capture on Windows
steps.body: Use the Windows Key + Shift + S shortcut to open the Snipping Tool and select your region.

steps.heading: Capture on macOS
steps.body: Hold Command + Shift + 4 to map out a custom crosshair selection area.

steps.heading: Mark up your capture
steps.body: Click the desktop preview thumbnail to open editing tools, then use highlighters or cropping to clean up the image.

steps.heading: Paste it into Teams
steps.body: Click into your Teams message compose field and press Ctrl+V or Command+V to paste the screenshot, then hit send.

faq.question: Do I need a separate app to take a Teams screenshot?
faq.answer: No, native OS shortcuts like the Windows Snipping Tool or macOS's Command+Shift+4 handle it directly.

faq.question: Can I blur out sensitive information before sharing?
faq.answer: Yes, the built-in markup tools let you highlight or blur specific areas like private email columns before sending.

faq.question: Why does my screenshot show a black screen for shared content?
faq.answer: This can happen due to graphics acceleration settings blocking captured application frames — adjusting those settings usually resolves it.

faq.question: Can I paste a screenshot directly into a chat without saving it first?
faq.answer: Yes, capturing to the clipboard lets you paste directly into the Teams message field with Ctrl+V or Command+V.
